title: 2026 Criminal Defence Direct (CDD)
body: "Latest Updates  26 August 2025 - Publication of a notice to amend the 2026 CDD Procurement Process  We have today issued an amendment notice to the 2026 CDD Procurement Process.  The notice can be found in the ‘documents’ section at the top of this page.  Applicants should download the latest version of the CDD Costs Breakdown Assessment Proforma from the e-Tendering System  Previous Updates  12 August 2025 - Publication of a notice to amend the 2026 CDD Procurement Process  We have today issued an amendment notice to the 2026 CDD Procurement Process.  The notice can be found in the ‘documents’ section at the top of this page.  Applicants should read the latest version of the Criminal Defence Direct Contract 2026: Standard Terms  6 August 2025 - Publication of the Procurement of Criminal Defence Direct Services Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)  We have today published the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) document relating to the Procurement of Criminal Defence Direct Services from 4 May 2026.  The FAQ can be found in the ‘documents’ section at the top of this page.  The deadline for submitting a tender through this procurement process is 17:00 on 28 August 2025.  16 July 2025 - Criminal Defence Direct (CDD) tender opens  Today the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) is inviting tenders for two new CDD Contracts to deliver 24/7 telephone-based legal advice from 4 May 2026. These services support individuals detained at police stations in England and Wales for non-indictable offences.  Key Dates   Tender opens: 16 July 2025 Deadline for questions: 30 July 2025 (17:00) TUPE data request deadline: 30 July 2025 (17:00) Tender submission deadline: 28 August 2025 (17:00)   Who Can Apply  The tender is open to any organisation that meets the LAA’s Conditions of Participation.  How to Apply  Applicants must submit a complete tender via two platforms:  1) Central Digital Platform (CDP) for supplier registration and core information  2) LAA eTendering portal for the CDD Tender Response.  A share code linking the CDP response must be included in the Qualification envelope of the CDD Tender Response.  For full details of the procurement process please read the Application Guide which can be found in the ‘documents’ section above.  Contract documentation is available on the Criminal Defence Direct Contract 2026 page. "
